programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Classic film review series hosted by legends Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton. In this episode they review Yolngu Boy and interview director Stephen Johnson and actor John Sebastian Pilakui. Later, they review Walk the Talk and interview Shirley Barrett.

programme.title: A King in New York
programme.desc: A recently deposed European monarch from a fictional country seeks shelter in New York City where he becomes an accidental television celebrity. But when he supports a child with communist parents, he also gets wrongly accused.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: Some of the biggest film stars in history rose to fame during the 'golden age' of Hollywood, in the early and mid-20th century. We profile some of these stars who became household names.

programme.title: Funny Face
programme.desc: A fashion photographer (Fred Astaire) turns a Greenwich Village bookworm (Audrey Hepburn) into a Paris cover girl.

programme.title: Fist of Fury
programme.desc: Kung Fu pupil Chen Zhen returns to Shanghai for the funeral of his former teacher. When he discovers that his mentor was in fact murdered, Chen sets out to avenge his death.

programme.title: Game of Death
programme.desc: Bruce Lee challenges the underworld to a game of death. Lee plays a martial arts movie star who must fake his own death in order to find the people who are trying to kill him. The film includes classic footage of Lee fighting basketballer Kareem Abdul Jabar and nunchaku expert Dan Inosanto.

programme.title: Brief Encounter
programme.desc: Brief Encounter by Noel Coward is a touching tale of forbidden love between two married people, Laura and Alex, who must choose between passion and duty. Now remastered in HD.

programme.title: Broken Hill
programme.desc: A sheep rancher's son dreams of being accepted into the Sydney Conservatorium of Music. He has trouble finding an adequate band in the outback to perform his compositions, until he comes upon a talented prison choir.

programme.title: The Lady and the Highwayman
programme.desc: During one of his robberies, a highwayman, who steals from the rich and gives to the poor, falls in love with an aristocratic lady. Now, he is forced to choose between his true love or his true cause.

programme.title: Becoming Jane
programme.desc: In 1795, young Jane Austen defies family and convention for love with Irishman Tom Lefroy-risking security, reputation, and shaping the voice of a literary legend.

programme.title: Marie Antoinette
programme.desc: Marie Antoinette is an electrifying yet intimate re-telling of the turbulent life of history's favourite villainess. Kirsten Dunst portrays the ill-fated child princess who married France's young and indifferent King Louis XVI (Jason Schwartzman). Feeling isolated in a royal court rife with scandal and intrigue, Marie Antoinette defied both royalty and commoner by living like a rock star, which served only to seal her fate.

programme.title: Book of Love
programme.desc: Uptight English writer Henry is shocked to find his Mexican translator Maria has rewritten his book - adding plenty of sex. Sparks fly in this fun, cross - cultural romantic comedy.

programme.title: Phantom Thread
programme.desc: In 1950s London, a brilliant dressmaker whose orderly life unravels when he falls for a strong-willed muse. Phantom Thread weaves love, power, and obsession.

programme.title: Empire
programme.desc: A wealthy free Black woman in the Danish West Indies maintains a complex bond with her enslaved housekeeper as tensions rise and rumours of rebellion spread, forcing both women to confront loyalty, power, and survival under colonial rule.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Actors discuss the Australian comedy Around the World in 80 Ways. Lina Wertmuller talks on feminism and language, and David Hannay on making Mapantsula in South Africa.

programme.title: The Lady and the Highwayman
programme.desc: During one of his robberies, a highwayman, who steals from the rich and gives to the poor, falls in love with an aristocratic lady. Now, he is forced to choose between his true love or his true cause.

programme.title: Becoming Jane
programme.desc: In 1795, young Jane Austen defies family and convention for love with Irishman Tom Lefroy-risking security, reputation, and shaping the voice of a literary legend.

programme.title: Phantom Thread
programme.desc: In 1950s London, a brilliant dressmaker whose orderly life unravels when he falls for a strong-willed muse. Phantom Thread weaves love, power, and obsession.

programme.title: Empire
programme.desc: A wealthy free Black woman in the Danish West Indies maintains a complex bond with her enslaved housekeeper as tensions rise and rumours of rebellion spread, forcing both women to confront loyalty, power, and survival under colonial rule.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: Some of the biggest film stars in history rose to fame during the 'golden age' of Hollywood, in the early and mid-20th century. We profile some of these stars who became household names.

programme.title: A King in New York
programme.desc: A recently deposed European monarch from a fictional country seeks shelter in New York City where he becomes an accidental television celebrity. But when he supports a child with communist parents, he also gets wrongly accused.

programme.title: Somewhere in Time
programme.desc: A young writer becomes obsessed with a mysterious actress and, through self-hypnosis, travels back in time to meet her.

programme.title: The Guernsey Literary Society
programme.desc: In post-war England, a writer forms an unexpected bond with the quirky members of a book club on Guernsey, uncovering their stories of resilience and love during the Nazi occupation.

programme.title: Enigma
programme.desc: During World War II, the British authorities discover that Nazi U-boats have unexpectedly changed the code of their Enigma machines, thus endangering the lives of thousands of Allied sailors. They enlist the help of Tom Jericho, a brilliant young code breaker. But Tom also needs to solve the mystery surrounding the woman he loves, who is suspected of being a spy.

programme.title: An Angel at My Table
programme.desc: The true story of gifted writer Janet Frame, who overcame tragedy and hardship through her talent, gaining success and happiness. Directed by Jane Campion.

programme.title: John Wick
programme.desc: John Wick, a former hitman grieving his true love, is forced to return to action after his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, to exact revenge.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Classic film review series hosted by legends Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton. In this episode they review The Contender, Miss Congeniality, Dracula 2000, The Exorcist and The Girl Next Door. Later, they interview actor Anthony La Paglia.
